zh.lifeSummary: 當Ralph St Leger於1430年出生在英國英格蘭肯特武爾可姆時，他的父親John St Leger31歲，母親Margery Donnet26歲。他和Anne Prophett至少有3個兒子和3個女兒。他去世於1470年11月11日在英國肯特武爾可姆享年40歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭肯特武爾可姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Ralph St Leger于1430年出生在英国英格兰肯特武爾可姆时，他的父亲John St Leger31岁，母亲Margery Donnet26岁。他和Anne Prophett至少有3个儿子和3个女儿。他去世于1470年11月11日在英国肯特武爾可姆享年40岁，埋葬在英国英格兰肯特武爾可姆。

nameMeanings.name: St
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized spelling of French   St  .   Denis  .

variants.name: Denis
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Camille, Jacques, Armand, Marcel, Andre, Pierre, Serge, Yvan, Alphonse, Edouard, Francois, Gaetane.  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Jorge, Roberto, Angel, Francisco, Joaquin, Juan, Manuel, Miguel, Ramon, Rolando.

French, Walloon, Breton, West Indian (mainly Haiti), German, Dutch, English, and Portuguese; Spanish and Galician (Denís): from the personal name Denis, Spanish and Galician Denís, from Latin Dionysius ‘(follower) of Dionysos’ (see Dennis ). Compare Dennee .
Ukrainian and Rusyn: variant transliteration of   Denys  , a cognate of 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Leger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Camille, Lucien, Pierre, Yvon, Antoine, Aurele, Emile, Jacques, Marcel, Zoel, Adelard.

French (Léger) and English: from the ancient Germanic personal name Leudgari, composed of the elements leud ‘people’ and gari (from garwa) ‘ready, eager’ or gari ‘spear’ and Latinized as Leodegar(ius). The name was borne by a 7th-century Christian saint, bishop of Autun, whose fame contributed to the popularity of the name in France. See also Ledger and St. Leger .
French (Léger): nickname from léger ‘light, superficial’.

variants.name: Lege
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alphie, Emile, Gaston, Henri, Leonce, Minus, Numa, Pierre, Rodolph.
German: topographic name meaning ‘swamp’.
Altered form of French Léger (see Leger ).

variants.name: Legere
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Armand, Normand, Aurele, Jacques, Adelard, Alban, Antoine, Desire, Eloi, Monique, Yvon.
French (Légère): nickname from légère, a feminine form of Léger ‘light, superficial’ (see Leger 2).
Altered form of French   Leger   1 or 2.

variants.name: Lougee
meanings: Channel Islands: altered form of Laugee, a variant of French Lauger, itself either a variant of Léger (see Leger ) or a variant, with fused definite article l', of Auger .

History: The first recorded American bearer of this surname was John Lougee from the isle of Jersey. Born Jean Laugee and by trade a knitter, he was aged c. 18 or 19 when he emigrated to the USA in the late 1600s, settling in Exeter, NH, where he became known as John Lougee, marrying Anne, the daughter of Colonel Moses Gilman.

variants.name: Ledger
meanings: English: from a Norman personal name, Leodegar, Old French Legier, of ancient Germanic origin (see Leger 1).
English: metonymic occupational name from Middle English legger ‘one who lays stone, a mason’, a derivative of Old English lecgan ‘to lay’.
Americanized form of French   Leger   1.

variants.name: St. Leger
meanings: French (Saint-Léger): habitational name from any of several places in various parts of France named Saint-Léger (from the dedication of their churches to Saint Leodegar, in French St. Léger, the martyred 7th-century bishop of Autun), or a nickname with the prefix Saint as an (ironic) attachment to the personal name or surname Léger (see Leger 1).

Haitian (also Saint-Léger): from Saint-Léger, a nickname, ornamental name, or personal name composed of the French prefix Saint and the personal name Léger (see Leger 1), or perhaps a habitational name from a place in Haiti called Saint-Léger (compare 1 above).
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Saint-Léger in La Manche or Saint-Léger-aux-Bois in Seine-Maritime, France (see 1 above).

nameMeanings.name: Ralph
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Norman French name, Raulf, a contracted form of the Germanic personal name Radulf, derived from rād ‘counsel’ + wulf ‘wolf’. The spelling with -ph is due to classical influence in the 18th century.

variants.name: Ralf
meanings: From a Norman French name, Raulf, a contracted form of the Germanic personal name Radulf, derived from rād ‘counsel’ + wulf ‘wolf’. The spelling with -ph is due to classical influence in the 18th century.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Ralph St Leger was born in 1430, in Ulcombe, Kent, England, United Kingdom, his father, John St Leger, was 31 and his mother, Margery Donnet, was 26. He had at least 3 sons and 3 daughters with Anne Prophett. He died on 11 November 1470, in Ulcombe, Kent, England, at the age of 40, and was buried in Ulcombe, Kent, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ralph St Leger est né en 1430, à Ulcombe, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John St Leger, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Margery Donnet, avait 26 ans. Il a eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles avec Anne Prophett. Il est décédé le 11 novembre 1470, à Ulcombe, Kent, Angleterre, à l’âge de 40 ans, et a été enterré à Ulcombe, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Ralph St Leger 1430 in Ulcombe, Kent, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater John St Leger 31 und seine Mutter Margery Donnet 26. Er hatte mit Anne Prophett mindestens 3 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 11. November 1470 im Alter von 40 Jahren in Ulcombe, Kent, England und wurde in Ulcombe, Kent, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Ralph St Leger è nato il nel 1430 nel Ulcombe, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, John St Leger, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Margery Donnet, aveva 26 anni. Lui avuto almeno 3 figli e 3 figlie con Anne Prophett. Morì il 11 novembre 1470, nel Ulcombe, Kent, England, all'età di 40 anni, e fu sepolto a Ulcombe, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Ralph St Legerが1430年にイギリスイギリスケントウルコンベで生まれた時，父John St Legerは31歳，母Margery Donnetは26歳であった。彼はAnne Prophettとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。彼は1470年11月11日にイギリスケントウルコンベで40歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスケントウルコンベにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Ralph St Leger(이)가 1430년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 울코브에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John St Leger(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Margery Donnet(은)는 26세였다. 그는 Anne Prophett(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1470년 11월 11일 영국 켄트 울코브에서 40세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 울코브에 묻혔다.

pt.1470: de 10 de janeiro de 1470 a 9 de janeiro de 1471
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Ralph St Leger nasceu em 1430, em Ulcombe, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, John St Leger, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Margery Donnet, tinha 26 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 3 filhas com Anne Prophett. Ele faleceu em 11 de novembro de 1470, em Ulcombe, Kent, Inglaterra, com 40 anos, e foi sepultado em Ulcombe, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ralph St Leger родился в 1430 г. в местности улкомб, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени John St Leger было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Margery Donnet, 26. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери от Anne Prophett. Он умер 11 ноября 1470 г. в местности улкомб, кэнт, Англия в возрасте 40 лет и был похоронен в местности улкомб, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания.
